talk_title: Keynotes 3 — Ayoung Kim, Luca Carlone, Maren Bennewitz, Timothy Barfoot
speaker: Ayoung Kim; Luca Carlone; Maren Bennewitz; Timothy Barfoot
speaker_affiliation: Seoul National University; MIT; Bonn; Toronto
tc_relevance_reason: Strongest single keynote block of the week for navigation/state-estimation TC members. Carlone (certifiable perception + planning), Bennewitz (humanoid nav and planning), Barfoot (state estimation for field robotics), and Kim (SLAM) — all senior figures whose work is squarely TC scope. Run across the convention for this one.

speaker: Barbara Mazzolai
tc_relevance_reason: Bio-inspired and soft robotics plenary. TC-adjacent — Mazzolai's work pushes the morphological-computation angle which intersects with control of underactuated/compliant systems but isn't direct planning/control methodology. Worth attending as the day's community moment.

talk_title: Panels 4 — Surviving the Paper Deluge: A One-Year Study in Learning from Demonstration
speaker: TBD
tc_relevance_reason: Meta-panel on navigating the LfD literature explosion. Not TC-core content but the framing (how does a research community absorb a flood of new methods?) is relevant for any TC member tracking the learning-augmented-control space.

talk_title: Keynotes 4 — Jeannette Bohg, Kento Kawaharazuka, Nikos Tsagarakis, Yuke Zhu
speaker: Jeannette Bohg; Kento Kawaharazuka; Nikos Tsagarakis; Yuke Zhu
tc_relevance_reason: Strong manipulation and humanoid lineup. Tsagarakis on humanoid control is direct TC content; Bohg on perception-for-action and Zhu on robot learning sit at the learning/control boundary that the TC increasingly cares about. Kawaharazuka's musculoskeletal humanoids are more system-design but interesting. Solid block for manipulation and humanoid TC members.
